revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ishiya Kofun]][[ja:石屋古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Ishiya Kofun | native_name =石屋古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Ishiya-Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ishiya Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ishiya Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.450833333333335|N|133.10194444444446|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Matsue|qid=Q207321|1=Matsue}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|square kofun|qid=Q11504353|1=Square Kofun}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|27|03|N|133|06|07|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ishiya Kofun'''|石屋古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located on the border of the Yata-chō and Higashitsuda-chō neighborhoods of the city of {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|qid=Q207321|lt=Matsue|1=Matsue}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139377|title=石屋古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is one of the largest rectangular tombs in the San'in region and is in good preservation.
==Overview== The Ishiya Kofun is located on the south bank of the Ōhashi River, which connects {{ill|Lake Shinji|en|Lake Shinji|qid=Q1151248|lt=Lake Shinji|1=Lake Shinji}} and {{ill|Nakaumi|en|Nakaumi|qid=Q1077275|lt=Nakaumi|1=Nakaumi}}, on a hill about 30 meters above sea level, approximately 2.5 kilometers east-southeast of central Matsue. This area is a key point for water transportation, and an ancient ferry named the "Asakanwatari" that appears in the ''[[Fudoki|Izumo-no-kuni Fudoki]]'' was at this location.
This rectangular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun||}}-style Kofun, approximately 37.5 meters by 42 meters, and approximately 7.5 meters in height and is orientated to the southwest. It was discovered in 1978 during construction of a residential area. It has a dry moat over 10 meters wide on the north and south sides. and smaller raised rectangular structures overhanging the moat exist in the north and south. While the northern structure is located on the central axis, the southern structure is located on the west side of the foot of the mound. Such an asymmetry is unusual. Another unusual feature is on the north side: about 5.5 meters away from the foot of the Kofun, there is a row of stones extending in an east–west direction, the purpose of which is unknown. The structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is uncertain as it has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], but it is a pit-type vertical stone chamber.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The mound was built in two stages, with ''[[fukiishi]]'' on the slope, and cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'' on the first stage. The ''fukiishi'' is {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|qid=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}}, the same stone which was used in the construction of the walls of [[Matsue Castle]]. Cylindrical ''haniwa'' were also lined up around the top of the structure, and inside these ''haniwa'' smaller figurative ''haniwa'' in the shapes of horses, people, shields, etc. were discovered, as well as {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pots and utensil stands. Some figurative ''haniwa'' are coated with red or green pigments, and many are in a style unique to this site. From the ''haniwa'' and Sue pottery items, it is believed that the tumulus was built in the he late 5th century, or the middleof the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Traditionally, it was thought that ''haniwa'' with a variety of shapes appeared in large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tombs|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} in the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] in the 5th century, but these ''haniwa'' have led the Shimane Prefectural Ancient Culture Center to state that the powerful clans of {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|qid=Q582846|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo Province}} may have been involved in the construction of large Kofuns in the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]], such as the [[Mozu Tombs|Daisen Kofun]] (the legendary tomb of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}), where similar clay figures of the same period have been excavated.
The tumulus is located next to {{ill|Japan National Route 9|en|Japan National Route 9|qid=Q1055522|lt=Japan National Route 9|1=Japan National Route 9}}, about 4.2 kilometers (10 minutes by car) from {{ill|Matsue Station|en|Matsue Station|qid=Q1058652|lt=Matsue Station|1=Matsue Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|qid=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?id=1645&page=1&search_kbn=site&act=&sitename=石屋古墳&sitekana=&target=site Archaeological Database of Shimane home page]{{in lang|ja}}
Ishiya Kofun
